_note: One-time market snapshot taken 2026-07-20 from Rory's ICE/Tradingeconomics screenshots. Power spot (114.72 GBP/MWh) and the gas futures curve/3-month series are read from source. The power `series` (1Y spot) and `range5y` are traced from the charts (shape-accurate, not tick-exact). Refresh by hand and redeploy; delete this file to hide the Market context tab.
headline: power at its highest since 2023; gas futures elevated into 2027
commentary: UK power is trading at its highest wholesale level since 2023 — when the Israel–Palestine conflict began — at £114.72/MWh, up on the day and with no sign of easing. Gas, which sets the UK power price most of the time, has kept climbing: front-month NBP futures are at their highest since the Iran conflict flared in March 2026 and the second-highest in two years, with elevated prices persisting right through to summer 2027. The market is at the top of its recent range and the forward curve offers no real relief until well into next year — exactly the environment a fixed contract is designed to take the sting out of.

gas.note: NBP gas futures at their highest since the Iran conflict (Mar 2026) — second-highest in two years — with elevated prices persisting through to summer 2027.
